name: Batch
description: Plan and execute parallel, repeatable migrations across code, docs, research artifacts, ops configs, or mixed task sets.
prompt:
Batch workflow.

Goal
- Execute repeatable work across multiple targets in parallel with clear checkpoints.

Inputs
- Objective: {{objective}}
- Domain: {{domain}}
- Parallelism: {{parallel}}
- External effects policy: {{external}}

Execution
1) Identify migration targets and split work into independent units.
2) Build a concise execution plan with ordering, dependencies, and risk notes.
3) Execute units in parallel up to {{parallel}} workers when safe.
4) Validate each unit before marking done (tests/checks/lint/quality checks as applicable).
5) Aggregate results into a single summary with per-unit status and blockers.

Safety defaults
- Local/file changes may execute automatically.
- External side effects must follow External effects policy:
  - confirm: draft actions and require explicit confirmation before execution.
  - execute: execute external actions when tool policies allow.
  - none: do not execute external actions.
- For code-domain runs: stop at branch/worktree + verified summary. Do not auto-open PRs unless user explicitly asks.

Output
- Batch plan
- Per-target execution results
- Validation outcomes
- Blockers and rollback notes
- Recommended next batch

parameters.name: objective
parameters.description: Batch objective to execute across multiple targets

routing.useWhen: Use when the user asks to run parallelizable migrations or repeated transformations across many independent targets in any domain.
routing.dontUseWhen: Do not use for single-target quality polishing (use simplify) or when the request is purely conceptual with no execution intent.
routing.outputs: A parallel execution plan, per-target outcomes, validations, and explicit next actions.
routing.successCriteria: Work is partitioned, executed with bounded parallelism, validated per target, and summarized with clear blockers/safety boundaries.

positive: Use /batch migrate src/ from Solid to React.
Run /batch to update all policy docs to the new template.
Batch-convert these reports and validate each output.
Apply this config migration across all environments in parallel.
negative: Clean up this one function for readability.
Improve this single memo's tone and clarity.
Discuss migration options without executing any changes.
Use simplify for one-off quality tuning instead of batch.
